center.name: 美国
center.summary: 美国是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联58种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、黑嘴天鹅、白尾雷鸟等58种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Bay-breasted warblers breed in the boreal spruce-fir forests of eastern and central Canada (Newfoundland, west to northeastern British Columbia), as well as the far northeast of the United States. The species winters in the wet lowland forests of northeastern South America, the Caribbean, and southern Central America, and may be seen during spring and fall migration across the eastern half of the United States in a variety of vegetative communities

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Mexico and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.1) |last1=Gill |first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Most winter in Mexico, while some fly farther to the north to the southwestern region of the US

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Cave swallows are found in Mexico and the Greater Antilles, with fall and winter vagrants reaching the east and Gulf Coasts of the U.S. Breeding colonies occur in south-eastern New Mexico, Texas, Florida, the Greater Antilles, portions of southern Mexico, and along the west coast of South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The majority of the population breeds in forests of the Appalachian mountains, with smaller numbers in forests throughout the United States and Canada

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|An illustration of two chestnut-backed chickadees by Louis Agassiz Fuertes It is found in the Pacific Northwest of the United States and western Canada, from southeastern Alaska to southwestern California

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Cocos booby are found in the East Pacific Ocean, breeding in Costa Rica, Nicaragua, Mexico, French Overseas Territories, Colombia, and the United States. It is also found offshore of Guatemala, El Salvador, and Panama

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The common ground dove (Columbina passerina) is a small bird that inhabits the southern United States, parts of Central America, the Caribbean and northern South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: ridgwayi in Honduras | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Glaucidium | species = brasilianum | authority = (Gmelin, JF, 1788) | range_map = Glaucidium brasilianum map.svg | range_map_caption = Distribution of ferruginous pygmy owl }} The ferruginous pygmy owl (Glaucidium brasilianum) is a small owl that breeds in south-central Arizona and southern Texas in the United States, south through Mexico and Central America, to South America into Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay and Argentina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The genus name Sterna is derived from Old English "stearn", "tern", and forsteri commemorates the naturalist Johann Reinhold Forster. It breeds inland in North America in the northern United States and southern Canada, and migrates south to winter in the southern United States, Mexico, the Caribbean, and northern Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The gray-crowned rosy finch or gray-crowned rosy-finch (Leucosticte tephrocotis) is a species of passerine bird in the family Fringillidae native to Alaska, western Canada, and the north-western United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, and the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The grey silky-flycatcher (Ptiliogonys cinereus) is a species of bird in the family Ptiliogonatidae. It is found in Guatemala and Mexico and as a vagrant in Texas, United States.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The hairy woodpecker inhabits mature deciduous forests in the Bahamas, Canada, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, and the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in the Americas, including the Rio Grande Valley of Texas in the United States, Mexico, the Caribbean, Central America, and tropical South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Other birds migrate to the southern United States and Mexico; in Canada, they are found in southern Ontario

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: lawrencei | synonyms = Carduelis lawrencei }} Lawrence's goldfinch (Spinus lawrencei) is a small songbird of erratic distribution that breeds in California and Baja California and winters in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The least flycatcher inhabits the Eastern Rockies of Canada, and the Central-North and Northeastern United-States. During winter, they migrate to Central America and establish themselves from Mexico to Panama. The least flycatcher lives in aspen clusters, orchards, shade trees and open woods. They breed in deciduous or mixed forests and occasionally in coniferous groves. They tend to prefer breeding sites near clearing or edges but can also nest in dry woods. They spend the winter in Central America where they nest in forest edges and second growth. The least flycatcher also made its way in the open country

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It winters in the southeastern United States; as far west as central Texas and as far north as central Illinois and Missouri.Manitoba Naturalists Society (Holland, G.E., C.E

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Louisiana waterthrush breeds in eastern North America from southernmost Canada and south through the eastern United States, excluding Florida and the coast

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The northern pygmy owl is native to Canada, the United States, and Mexico. Their habitat includes temperate, subtropical and tropical moist forest, savanna, and wetlands. In Oregon and Washington they are known to nest and forage in the center of dense, continuous forests, near streams. An example of their habitat is Forest Park in Portland, Oregon, USA

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the northern part of North America, in Alaska, Canada, and the northern United States; it winters in Florida, Central America, the West Indies, Venezuela, Colombia, and Ecuador.{{Cite journal |last1=Smith |first1=Joseph A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: On the South American mainland it is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, French Guiana, Guyana, Paraguay, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela. It has been recorded as a vagrant on Trinidad and in the U.S. states of Texas and Virginia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are migratory and winter in coastal areas of the southern United States, the Caribbean and much of South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The snail kite breeds in tropical South America, the Caribbean, and central and southern Florida in the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Migratory, it spends the winters in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It ranges in the Eastern Pacific Ocean north to southern California in the United States and south to 10°N latitude

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The western screech owl is native to Canada, United States, Mexico, Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Costa Rica, and Nicaragua. Its habitat includes temperate forests, subtropical and tropical montane forests, shrubland, desert, rural fields, and even suburban parks and gardens

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the United States, it is found only in the Florida Keys, Everglades, and the southern tip of mainland Florida. They will often spend the winter in the Caribbean islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This bird, the largest of the phalaropes, breeds in the prairies of North America in western Canada and the western United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Northern rough-winged swallow photographed in central Maine, the northeastern limit of the species' breeding range. The northern rough-winged swallow is native to Bahamas, Belize, Canada, Cayman Islands, Costa Rica, Cuba, El Salvador, Guatemala, Haiti, Honduras, Jamaica, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Puerto Rico, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, Turks and Caicos Islands, and the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The bobolink breeds in the summer in the United States and Canada, with most of the summer range in the northern U.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are long distance migrants and winter in coastal South America, with some going to the southern United States and the Caribbean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in the South Pacific, nesting on rocky islets and cliffs off tropical oceanic islands in the Austral, Tuamotu, and Pitcairn groups. It was not until the 1980s that it was determined that these petrels might be regular visitors far offshore of the west coast of North America. It has been recorded off the coast of the Hawaiian Islands and well off the Pacific Coast of the United States and in the southern Gulf of Alaska. Most reports of Murphy's petrels are over 64 km offshore and the species reportedly has one of the greatest foraging ranges of any breeding seabird{{Cite journal|url=https://www.int-res.com/prepress/m12244.html|doi = 10.3354/meps12244|title = Escaping the oligotrophic gyre?

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.1 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Across the northern portion of the Atlantic, this gull is distributed in Iceland, the Faroe Islands, southern Greenland and on the Atlantic coasts of Canada and the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|220x220px|Fighting, Madagascar The common myna is native to Asia, with its initial home range spanning Iran, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Myanmar, Malaysia, Singapore, peninsular Thailand, Indochina, Japan (both mainland Japan and the Ryukyu Islands) and China. thumb|left|A common myna as an invasive species in Gülhane Park, Turkey thumb|Visiting a home garden in Colombo, [[Sri Lanka]]The common myna has been introduced to many other parts of the world such as Canada, Australia, Turkey, Israel, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Fiji, the United States (South Florida only), South Africa, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cayman Islands, Islands in the Indian Ocean (the Seychelles – from which it was subsequently eradicated at great expense,{{Cite journal| journal= PLOS ONE|first1=Thomas|last1=Evans|first2=Elena|last2=Angulo|first3=Corey J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: platycercus }} The broad-tailed hummingbird (Selasphorus platycercus) is a medium-sized hummingbird species found in highland regions from western United States and Western Canada to Mexico and Guatemala.{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sklz7tAUa4IC&q=427|title=A Guide to the Birds of Mexico and Northern Central America, page 427|last1=Howell|first1=Steve N

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The calliope hummingbird ( ; Selasphorus calliope) is the smallest bird native to the United States and Canada. It has a western breeding range mainly from California to British Columbia, and migrates to the Southwestern United States, Mexico, and Central America for its wintering grounds. The calliope hummingbird is the smallest known long-distance bird migrant, completing migrations twice per year of some . It was previously considered the only member of the genus Stellula (meaning little star), but research evidence suggests its existing placement in the genus Selasphorus.{{cite journal |author1=McGuire, J.A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in coniferous woods across Alaska, the western mountains of the United States, Canada, and in subarctic Fennoscandia and across the Palearctic to Siberia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to Canada and the United States, China, Mongolia, the Russian Federation, Kazakhstan, the Czech Republic, Finland, Norway, Sweden, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. It primarily occupies subalpine and subarctic habitats such as sparse pine and birch forests, thickets with willow and alder trees, heather moors, tundra and mountain slopes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Belize, Guatemala, Mexico, and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in southern Europe, North Africa, northern India, northern Pakistan, China, and Japan. It frequently strays into Alaska during migration and there are scattered records across the northern United States and southern Canada. The global population of bramblings is about 100 to 200 million, with a decreasing trend. Open coniferous or birch woodland is favoured for breeding

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is a permanent resident of high altitudes on or above the tree line and is native to Alaska and the mountainous parts of Canada and the western United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: At minimum population, in the 1950s, it was largely restricted to Alaska, the Aleutian Islands, northern and eastern Canada, and Florida. From 1966 to 2015 bald eagle numbers increased substantially throughout its winter and breeding ranges, and as of 2018 the species nests in every continental state and province in the United States and Canada. The majority of bald eagles in Canada are found along the British Columbia coast while large populations are found in the forests of Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba and Ontario. Bald eagles also congregate in certain locations in winter

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Couch|Couch]], 1854 | range_map = Corvus cryptoleucos map.jpg | range_map_caption = Distribution map }} The Chihuahuan raven (Corvus cryptoleucus) is a species in the family Corvidae that is native to the United States and Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red-crowned amazon (Amazona viridigenalis), also known as the red-crowned parrot, green-cheeked amazon or Mexican red-headed parrot, is an endangered amazon parrot native to northeastern Mexico and possibly southern Texas in the United States. A 1994 study estimated wild populations of between 2,000 and 4,300 mature individuals; the IUCN Red List considers it a globally endangered species with a decreasing population. The main threats to the native bird's survival are the illegal export of trapped birds from Mexico to the United States for the pet trade and the destruction of their natural habitat, the lowland forests of northeastern Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Mexico and the southwestern United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American flamingo breeds in South America (in the Galápagos Islands of Ecuador, coastal Colombia and Venezuela, and northern Brazil), in the West Indies (Trinidad and Tobago, Cuba, Jamaica, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), The Bahamas, the Virgin Islands, and the Turks and Caicos Islands), and tropical and subtropical areas of continental North America (along the northern coast of the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico, and formerly southern Florida in the United States)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The fulvous whistling duck or fulvous tree duck (Dendrocygna bicolor) is a species of whistling duck that breeds across the world's tropical regions in much of Mexico and South America, the West Indies, the southern United States, sub-Saharan Africa and the Indian subcontinent

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The prothonotary warbler mostly breeds in hardwood swamps in extreme southeastern Ontario and the eastern United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pin-tailed whydah occurs in much of sub-Saharan Africa, favouring grassland, scrubs and savannah, also coming into parks and gardens. In Portugal it is established in the region around Aveiro, with observations occurring north and south of it. It has been introduced to southern California, with a sizable population existing there, as well as Puerto Rico and Singapore. It can be kept as a domesticated pet in the United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Ringing & Migration, 15(2), 104-106. On evidence, many of the females that vacate Norway and even Fennoscandia come to winter in Great Britain (the female migrants here being up to 3.5 times more common during winter than male migrants in one study). Two birds in ringing studies that were recorded in late winter in Germany were observed to return for the summer to central Russia (near Yaroslavl, away) and to eastern Russia (near Kazan, away). Birds that breed in central Asia have been recorded to winter in a wide variety of locales, including the Egyptian Nile valley, Pakistan, northern India and southern China. In North America, migrants generally sourced from Canada and the Upper Midwest winter almost anywhere in the remaining United States, however they will irruptively migrate as far as [[Georgia (U.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date = 2015|journal = Contemporary Problems of Ecology|doi = 10.1134/S1995425515030117|volume=8|issue = 3|pages=309–316| doi-access=free| bibcode=2015CPrEc...8..309R }} This species is found in the high Arctic tundra of North America, Ellesmere Island, Iceland, higher mountains of Scotland, Norway, Russia, North Greenland, Siberia, Novaya Zemlya, and Franz Josef Land. During the winter, this bird migrates to the circumglobal northern temperate zone including the south of Canada, the northern United States, the coasts and plains of northern Europe in Great Britain, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Ukraine, and east to central Asia. During the last ice age, the snow bunting was widespread throughout continental Europe.{{cite journal | last1 = Tomek | first1 = T

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-bellied sapsucker (Sphyrapicus varius) is a medium-sized woodpecker that breeds in Canada and the northeastern United States

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the winter, trumpeter swans migrate to the southern tier of Canada, the eastern part of the northwest United States (especially to the Red Rock Lakes area of Montana), and the northern area of the Puget Sound region of Washington state; migratory populations have even been observed as far south as Pagosa Springs, Colorado

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Black-chinned hummingbirds are found in most of the western United States, reaching north into Canada in Alberta and British Columbia, east to Oklahoma, and as far south as Mexico. They can be found in mountains, woodlands, orchards, meadows, and chaparral habitats

links.evidence: platycercus }} The broad-tailed hummingbird (Selasphorus platycercus) is a medium-sized hummingbird species found in highland regions from western United States and Western Canada to Mexico and Guatemala.{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sklz7tAUa4IC&q=427|title=A Guide to the Birds of Mexico and Northern Central America, page 427|last1=Howell|first1=Steve N

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The calliope hummingbird ( ; Selasphorus calliope) is the smallest bird native to the United States and Canada. It has a western breeding range mainly from California to British Columbia, and migrates to the Southwestern United States, Mexico, and Central America for its wintering grounds. The calliope hummingbird is the smallest known long-distance bird migrant, completing migrations twice per year of some . It was previously considered the only member of the genus Stellula (meaning little star), but research evidence suggests its existing placement in the genus Selasphorus.{{cite journal |author1=McGuire, J.A

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in coniferous woods across Alaska, the western mountains of the United States, Canada, and in subarctic Fennoscandia and across the Palearctic to Siberia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is native to Canada and the United States, China, Mongolia, the Russian Federation, Kazakhstan, the Czech Republic, Finland, Norway, Sweden, Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. It primarily occupies subalpine and subarctic habitats such as sparse pine and birch forests, thickets with willow and alder trees, heather moors, tundra and mountain slopes

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Belize, Guatemala, Mexico, and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in southern Europe, North Africa, northern India, northern Pakistan, China, and Japan. It frequently strays into Alaska during migration and there are scattered records across the northern United States and southern Canada. The global population of bramblings is about 100 to 200 million, with a decreasing trend. Open coniferous or birch woodland is favoured for breeding

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is a permanent resident of high altitudes on or above the tree line and is native to Alaska and the mountainous parts of Canada and the western United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: At minimum population, in the 1950s, it was largely restricted to Alaska, the Aleutian Islands, northern and eastern Canada, and Florida. From 1966 to 2015 bald eagle numbers increased substantially throughout its winter and breeding ranges, and as of 2018 the species nests in every continental state and province in the United States and Canada. The majority of bald eagles in Canada are found along the British Columbia coast while large populations are found in the forests of Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba and Ontario. Bald eagles also congregate in certain locations in winter

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Couch|Couch]], 1854 | range_map = Corvus cryptoleucos map.jpg | range_map_caption = Distribution map }} The Chihuahuan raven (Corvus cryptoleucus) is a species in the family Corvidae that is native to the United States and Mexico

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The red-crowned amazon (Amazona viridigenalis), also known as the red-crowned parrot, green-cheeked amazon or Mexican red-headed parrot, is an endangered amazon parrot native to northeastern Mexico and possibly southern Texas in the United States. A 1994 study estimated wild populations of between 2,000 and 4,300 mature individuals; the IUCN Red List considers it a globally endangered species with a decreasing population. The main threats to the native bird's survival are the illegal export of trapped birds from Mexico to the United States for the pet trade and the destruction of their natural habitat, the lowland forests of northeastern Mexico

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Mexico and the southwestern United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, and the United States.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/hummingbirds/ |title=Hummingbirds |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.2 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The American flamingo breeds in South America (in the Galápagos Islands of Ecuador, coastal Colombia and Venezuela, and northern Brazil), in the West Indies (Trinidad and Tobago, Cuba, Jamaica, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), The Bahamas, the Virgin Islands, and the Turks and Caicos Islands), and tropical and subtropical areas of continental North America (along the northern coast of the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico, and formerly southern Florida in the United States)

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The fulvous whistling duck or fulvous tree duck (Dendrocygna bicolor) is a species of whistling duck that breeds across the world's tropical regions in much of Mexico and South America, the West Indies, the southern United States, sub-Saharan Africa and the Indian subcontinent

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The prothonotary warbler mostly breeds in hardwood swamps in extreme southeastern Ontario and the eastern United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The pin-tailed whydah occurs in much of sub-Saharan Africa, favouring grassland, scrubs and savannah, also coming into parks and gardens. In Portugal it is established in the region around Aveiro, with observations occurring north and south of it. It has been introduced to southern California, with a sizable population existing there, as well as Puerto Rico and Singapore. It can be kept as a domesticated pet in the United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Ringing & Migration, 15(2), 104-106. On evidence, many of the females that vacate Norway and even Fennoscandia come to winter in Great Britain (the female migrants here being up to 3.5 times more common during winter than male migrants in one study). Two birds in ringing studies that were recorded in late winter in Germany were observed to return for the summer to central Russia (near Yaroslavl, away) and to eastern Russia (near Kazan, away). Birds that breed in central Asia have been recorded to winter in a wide variety of locales, including the Egyptian Nile valley, Pakistan, northern India and southern China. In North America, migrants generally sourced from Canada and the Upper Midwest winter almost anywhere in the remaining United States, however they will irruptively migrate as far as [[Georgia (U.S

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |date = 2015|journal = Contemporary Problems of Ecology|doi = 10.1134/S1995425515030117|volume=8|issue = 3|pages=309–316| doi-access=free| bibcode=2015CPrEc...8..309R }} This species is found in the high Arctic tundra of North America, Ellesmere Island, Iceland, higher mountains of Scotland, Norway, Russia, North Greenland, Siberia, Novaya Zemlya, and Franz Josef Land. During the winter, this bird migrates to the circumglobal northern temperate zone including the south of Canada, the northern United States, the coasts and plains of northern Europe in Great Britain, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Ukraine, and east to central Asia. During the last ice age, the snow bunting was widespread throughout continental Europe.{{cite journal | last1 = Tomek | first1 = T

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The yellow-bellied sapsucker (Sphyrapicus varius) is a medium-sized woodpecker that breeds in Canada and the northeastern United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In the winter, trumpeter swans migrate to the southern tier of Canada, the eastern part of the northwest United States (especially to the Red Rock Lakes area of Montana), and the northern area of the Puget Sound region of Washington state; migratory populations have even been observed as far south as Pagosa Springs, Colorado

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Black-chinned hummingbirds are found in most of the western United States, reaching north into Canada in Alberta and British Columbia, east to Oklahoma, and as far south as Mexico. They can be found in mountains, woodlands, orchards, meadows, and chaparral habitats
